text: The limiting reagent in a reaction is the one that:
options: Is completely consumed first and limits the product
explanation: The limiting reagent is completely consumed first and determines the maximum amount of product formed.
name: Limiting Reagent
subject: che

text: The middle term in the expansion of \( (x+y)^8 \) is the:
explanation: For even \(n\), middle term is \( T_{\frac{n}{2}+1} = T_5 \) (5th term).
name: Middle Term
subject: maths

text: Enzymes are biological catalysts that are:
explanation: Enzymes are mostly globular proteins. Exception: ribozymes are catalytic RNA molecules. Highly specific, work at body temperature and neutral pH.
name: Nature of Enzymes
subject: che

text: The stability of chelate complexes compared to analogous non-chelate complexes is known as:
explanation: Chelate effect: chelate complexes are thermodynamically more stable than analogous non-chelate complexes due to increased entropy and ring formation.
name: Chelate Effect
subject: che

text: The slope of the \(x\)-axis is:
explanation: The \(x\)-axis is horizontal: slope \( = 0 \). The \(y\)-axis is vertical: slope undefined (\(\infty\)).
name: Slope of Axes
subject: maths

text: In \( \text{SF}_6 \), the hybridisation of S is:
explanation: \(\text{SF}_6\): 6 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) hybridisation → octahedral shape.
name: Hybridisation in SF₆
subject: che

text: Zener diode is used as:
options: Voltage regulator (operated in reverse breakdown region)
explanation: Zener diode: designed to operate in reverse breakdown at a fixed Zener voltage \(V_Z\). Output voltage stays constant despite input variation → voltage regulation.
name: Zener Diode Application
subject: phy

text: The area of parallelogram with adjacent sides \( \vec{a} \) and \( \vec{b} \) is:
explanation: Area of parallelogram \( = |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \). Area of triangle \( = \frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \).
name: Area Using Cross Product
subject: maths

text: The wave equation \( \frac{\partial^2 y}{\partial t^2} = v^2 \frac{\partial^2 y}{\partial x^2} \) is satisfied by:
options: Any wave function of form \( y = f(x \pm vt) \)
explanation: The general solution is \( y = f(x-vt) + g(x+vt) \) — any shape travelling in \(+x\) or \(-x\) direction.
name: Wave Equation
subject: phy

text: For a body on a rough horizontal surface, the limiting friction is:
explanation: Limiting static friction \( f_{s,max} = \mu_s N \). For any applied force \( F \leq f_{s,max} \): body remains static.
name: Limiting Friction
subject: phy

text: The equation of normal to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
explanation: Normal slope \( = -\frac{1}{f'(x_1)} \). Normal: \( y-y_1 = -\frac{1}{f'(x_1)}(x-x_1) \).
name: Equation of Normal
subject: maths

text: Electric field lines are drawn such that:
options: They start from negative and end at positive charges
They start from positive charges and end at negative charges
explanation: Electric field lines: originate from positive charges, terminate on negative charges. They never intersect, and their density indicates field strength.
name: Electric Field Lines
subject: phy

text: The Quotient Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}\left(\frac{u}{v}\right) = \)
explanation: Quotient Rule: \( \left(\frac{u}{v}\right)' = \frac{u'v - uv'}{v^2} \)
name: Quotient Rule
subject: maths

text: The orbital velocity of a satellite at height \(h\) above Earth is:
explanation: Orbital velocity: \( v_o = \sqrt{\frac{GM}{r}} = \sqrt{\frac{GM}{R+h}} \). For surface orbit (h≈0): \( v_o = \sqrt{gR} \approx 8\,\text{km/s} \).
name: Orbital Velocity
subject: phy

text: The real part of \( \frac{1}{1-\cos\theta + i\sin\theta} \) is:
explanation: After rationalizing and simplifying using half-angle identities, the real part equals \( \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Real Part Extraction
subject: maths

text: The equation \( \frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{y^2}{9}=1 \). Here \( a=4, b=3 \), so \( c = \)
explanation: \( c^2 = a^2-b^2 = 16-9 = 7 \Rightarrow c = \sqrt{7} \)
name: Foci of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: The number of ways \(n\) people can sit around a circular table is:
explanation: For circular arrangements, one position is fixed: \( (n-1)! \) ways.
name: Circular Permutation
subject: maths

text: The quantity of electricity required to deposit 1 mole of Cu (2+ ion) is:
explanation: \( \text{Cu}^{2+} + 2e^- \to \text{Cu} \). 2 moles of electrons needed = 2F = \( 2 \times 96500 = 193000 \) C.
name: Charge to Deposit Cu
subject: che

text: The general solution of \( \cos\theta = \cos\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = \cos\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = 2n\pi \pm \alpha,\ n \in \mathbb{Z} \)
name: General Solution cosθ=cosα
subject: maths

text: Angular momentum \(L\) of a rigid body is:
explanation: Angular momentum: \( L = I\omega \). Also \( L = mvr \) for a point mass. Units: kg m²/s.
name: Angular Momentum
subject: phy

text: The parallel axis theorem states:
explanation: Parallel axis theorem: \( I = I_{cm} + Md^2 \), where \(d\) = distance from CM axis to new parallel axis.
name: Parallel Axis Theorem
subject: phy

text: Which of the following has the smallest size?
explanation: All are isoelectronic (10 electrons). Higher nuclear charge → smaller size. Al³⁺ (Z=13) smallest, Na (Z=11) largest among ions.
name: Smallest Isoelectronic Size
subject: che

text: The average power dissipated in an AC circuit is:
explanation: Average power: \( P = V_{rms}I_{rms}\cos\phi = I_{rms}^2 R \). Also called true power (watts). Apparent power = \(V_{rms}I_{rms}\) (volt-ampere, VA).
name: Average Power in AC
subject: phy

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}\int_a^{g(x)}f(t)\,dt = \)
explanation: Leibniz rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}\int_a^{g(x)}f(t)dt = f(g(x))\cdot g'(x) \) (chain rule applied).
name: Leibniz Rule
subject: maths

text: The equation of tangent to \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) at point \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
explanation: Tangent at \((x_1,y_1)\): \( xx_1+yy_1=r^2 \). Replace \(x^2\to xx_1\) and \(y^2\to yy_1\).
name: Tangent at Point
subject: maths

text: Metallic character down a group:
explanation: Metallic character increases down a group (easier to lose electrons due to larger size, lower IE). Cs is most metallic alkali metal.
name: Metallic Character Trend
subject: che

text: The permittivity of free space \(\epsilon_0\) has value:
explanation: \( \epsilon_0 = 8.85\times10^{-12}\,\text{C}^2/(\text{N m}^2) \). Note: \( k = 1/4\pi\epsilon_0 \approx 9\times10^9\,\text{N m}^2/\text{C}^2 \).
name: Permittivity of Free Space
subject: phy

text: Adding an inert gas at constant volume to an equilibrium mixture:
explanation: At constant volume, adding inert gas doesn't change partial pressures of reactants/products → no shift in equilibrium.
name: Inert Gas at Constant Volume
subject: che

text: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution on aryl halides is facilitated by:
explanation: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution (SNAr): electron-withdrawing groups (–NO₂) ortho/para to leaving group stabilise the Meisenheimer complex intermediate.
name: Nucleophilic Aromatic Substitution
subject: che

text: The directrix of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2=4ax \): directrix is the vertical line \( x = -a \).
name: Directrix of Parabola
subject: maths

text: Stokes' law gives the viscous drag force on a sphere of radius \(r\) moving at speed \(v\) as:
explanation: Stokes' law: \( F = 6\pi\eta r v \). At terminal velocity: \( 6\pi\eta r v_t = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3(\rho - \sigma)g \).
name: Stokes' Law
subject: phy

text: In which case is work done against gravity zero?
options: Moving a body horizontally on a frictionless surface
explanation: Gravity is vertical. Horizontal displacement → \( W_{gravity} = mgh = 0 \) (no vertical displacement). Work by gravity = 0.
name: Zero Work by Gravity
subject: phy

text: The van der Waals equation for real gases is:
explanation: Van der Waals: \( \left(P+\frac{an^2}{V^2}\right)(V-nb)=nRT \). '\(a\)' corrects for intermolecular forces, '\(b\)' for volume.
name: Van der Waals Equation
subject: che

text: Kepler's third law states: \( T^2 \propto \)
explanation: Kepler's 3rd Law: \( T^2 \propto r^3 \) (semi-major axis). \( \frac{T_1^2}{T_2^2} = \frac{r_1^3}{r_2^3} \).
name: Kepler's Third Law
subject: phy

text: Number of ways to choose 3 students from a group of 8 is:
name: Selection
subject: maths

text: The energy of a photon with frequency \(\nu\) is:
explanation: Planck's equation: \( E = h\nu = \frac{hc}{\lambda} \), where \( h = 6.626 \times 10^{-34} \) J·s.
name: Planck's Equation
subject: che

text: Two circles are orthogonal if:
explanation: Orthogonality condition: \( 2g_1g_2+2f_1f_2 = c_1+c_2 \)
name: Orthogonal Circles
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \sin 30° = \)
explanation: Standard value: \( \sin 30° = \frac{1}{2} \)
name: sin 30°
subject: maths

text: The magnifying power of a simple microscope at near point (D=25 cm) is:
explanation: Simple microscope: \( M = 1 + D/f \) (image at near point). \( M = D/f \) (image at infinity). Near point \(D = 25\) cm.
name: Magnifying Power of Simple Microscope
subject: phy

text: In thin film interference, the condition for bright reflected light from film of thickness \(t\) and refractive index \(n\) is (both reflections at denser medium):
explanation: Thin film (same phase change at both surfaces): Constructive: \( 2nt = (2m-1)\lambda/2 \). Destructive: \( 2nt = m\lambda \). (One phase change at each interface.)
name: Thin Film Interference
subject: phy

text: The change of base formula: \( \log_a b = \)
explanation: Change of base: \( \log_a b = \frac{\log b}{\log a} = \frac{\ln b}{\ln a} \)
name: Change of Base
subject: maths

text: If \( \vec{a} = 2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) and \( \vec{b} = \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2\hat{k} \), then \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = \)
name: Dot Product Calculation
subject: maths

text: The probability of an impossible event is:
explanation: An impossible event has no outcomes, so \( P(\phi) = 0 \).
name: Impossible Event
subject: maths

text: The conjugate of \( z = \frac{2+3i}{4-5i} \) is:
explanation: First simplify: \( z = \frac{(2+3i)(4+5i)}{41} = \frac{-7+22i}{41} \). Conjugate flips sign of imaginary part: \( \bar{z} = \frac{-7-22i}{41} \). Wait — re-check: \( \frac{7-22i}{41} \) is \(\overline{z}\) after correct multiplication.
name: Conjugate of Complex Fraction
subject: maths

text: The standard equation of a parabola with axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Standard parabola (opens right): \( y^2 = 4ax \), focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \( x = -a \).
name: Parabola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: PAN (peroxyacetyl nitrate) is a component of:
explanation: PAN (CH₃CO–O–O–NO₂): formed in photochemical smog. Powerful lachrymator (causes eye tearing), damages plants, harms respiratory system.
name: PAN in Smog
subject: che

text: Hydrogen bonding is strongest in:
explanation: H-bonding strength depends on electronegativity and size of atom. F is most electronegative and smallest → strongest H-bond in HF.
name: Strongest Hydrogen Bond
subject: che

explanation: \( f(f(x)) = \frac{x-1}{x} \), \( f(f(f(x))) = \frac{1}{1-\frac{x-1}{x}} = x \). Period 3 composition.
name: Triple Composition
subject: maths

text: The equation \( x^3 - 1 = 0 \) has how many real roots?
name: Roots of x³=1
subject: maths

text: The order of the DE representing family of circles \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) (r arbitrary) is:
explanation: One arbitrary constant \(r\) → eliminate by differentiating once → order 1.
name: Order from Family of Curves
subject: maths

text: In an ionic crystal with NaCl structure, the coordination number of \( \text{Na}^+ \) is:
explanation: In NaCl structure: each Na⁺ is surrounded by 6 Cl⁻ (and vice versa). CN = 6:6.
name: NaCl Coordination Number
subject: che

text: Two events \(A\) and \(B\) are independent if:
explanation: Events are independent if and only if \( P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B) \).
name: Independent Events
subject: maths

text: The focus of parabola \( y^2 = 4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2 = 4ax \): vertex at origin, focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \(x=-a\).
name: Focus of Parabola
subject: maths

text: The conversion of phenol to cyclohexanol requires:
explanation: Phenol → cyclohexanol by catalytic hydrogenation: PhOH + 3H₂ (Pt/Pd, high pressure) → cyclohexanol. The aromatic ring is reduced.
name: Phenol to Cyclohexanol
subject: che

text: The relationship between \( \Delta G^\circ \) and \( E^\circ_{cell} \) is:
explanation: \( \Delta G^\circ = -nFE^\circ_{cell} \). At equilibrium: \( \Delta G = 0 \), \( E = 0 \). Also \( \Delta G^\circ = -RT\ln K \).
name: ΔG° and E°cell
subject: che

text: The mean of squared deviations from mean is called:
explanation: Variance = mean of squared deviations from mean. SD is its square root.
name: Variance Definition
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = a^x\ln a \). When \(a=e\): reduces to \(e^x\).
name: Derivative of aˣ
subject: maths

explanation: \( \tan^{-1}x + \cot^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{2} \) for all real \(x\).
name: tan⁻¹ + cot⁻¹ Identity
subject: maths

text: The power of a lens in dioptres (D) is:
explanation: Power: \( P = 1/f(\text{metres}) = 100/f(\text{cm}) \). Unit: dioptre (D). Converging lens: \(P > 0\). Diverging: \(P < 0\).
name: Power of Lens
subject: phy

text: Both roots of \( x^2 - 6x + k = 0 \) are positive if:
explanation: Need \( D \geq 0 \Rightarrow k \leq 9 \), sum \(=6>0\), product \(=k>0\). So \( 0 < k \leq 9 \). Strictly positive roots: \( 0 < k < 9 \).
name: Both Roots Positive
subject: maths

text: Crystal Field Theory explains the colour of coordination compounds based on:
options: Splitting of d orbitals by ligand field into different energy levels
Formation of covalent bonds with ligands
explanation: CFT: Ligands create an electric field that splits degenerate d orbitals (eg and t₂g in octahedral). d-d transitions between split levels cause colour absorption.
name: Crystal Field Theory
subject: che

text: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter is:
explanation: Solid sphere (diameter): \( I = \frac{2}{5}MR^2 \). Hollow sphere: \( \frac{2}{3}MR^2 \). Solid disc: \( \frac{1}{2}MR^2 \).
name: MOI of Solid Sphere
subject: phy

text: The radius of circular motion of a charged particle in a magnetic field is:
explanation: Circular motion in \(B\): centripetal force = magnetic force: \( \frac{mv^2}{r} = qvB \Rightarrow r = \frac{mv}{qB} \). Used in mass spectrometer.
name: Radius of Circular Motion in B
subject: phy

text: The general electronic configuration of transition elements (3d series) is:
explanation: 3d transition series: [Ar] 3d¹⁻¹⁰ 4s¹⁻². Exceptions: Cr ([Ar]3d⁵4s¹) and Cu ([Ar]3d¹⁰4s¹) due to extra stability of half-filled/fully-filled d.
name: Electronic Config of Transition Elements
subject: che

text: Which transition metal has the highest melting point?
explanation: Tungsten (W) has the highest melting point (3422°C) of all metals, due to strong metallic bonding (d-electron contribution).
name: Highest Melting Point Metal
subject: che

text: The half-life \(T_{1/2}\) of a radioactive substance is related to decay constant \(\lambda\) by:
explanation: Half-life: \( T_{1/2} = \ln 2/\lambda = 0.693/\lambda \). After \(n\) half-lives: \( N = N_0/2^n \).
name: Half-Life Formula
subject: phy

text: The energy stored in an inductor with inductance \(L\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Energy in inductor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}LI^2 \). Stored in magnetic field. Analogous to \( \frac{1}{2}CV^2 \) in capacitor.
name: Energy in Inductor
subject: phy

text: If \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = 5 \) and \( \alpha\beta = 2 \), then \( (\alpha - \beta)^2 = \)
explanation: \( (\alpha-\beta)^2 = \alpha^2+\beta^2 - 2\alpha\beta = 5 - 4 = 1 \)
name: Difference of Roots Squared
subject: maths

text: A fair die is rolled. The probability of getting a number \( > 4 \) is:
name: Simple Probability - Die
subject: maths

text: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B) \). If \( P(A)=0.3, P(B)=0.4, P(A \cap B)=0.1 \), then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
name: Addition Theorem
subject: maths

explanation: Set \( x=1 \): \( \frac{1+1}{1 \cdot 0+...} \to 2 = C \cdot \frac{1}{1} \Rightarrow C = 2 \)
name: Repeated Factor Constant
subject: maths

text: \( \cosh 2x = \)
explanation: All three are valid: \( \cosh 2x = 2\cosh^2x-1 = 1+2\sinh^2x = \cosh^2x+\sinh^2x \)
name: cosh 2x Identities
subject: maths

text: The rank of a \( 3 \times 3 \) identity matrix \( I_3 \) is:
explanation: The identity matrix is full rank. For \( I_3 \): rank \( = 3 \) since \( |I_3| = 1 \neq 0 \).
name: Rank of Identity Matrix
subject: maths

text: The normal form of a line is \( x\cos\alpha + y\sin\alpha = p \). Here \(p\) represents:
explanation: In normal form, \(p\) is the perpendicular distance from the origin to the line, always positive.
name: Normal Form
subject: maths

text: The angle between \( \vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j} \) and \( \vec{b}=\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) is:
explanation: \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = 0+1+0=1 \). \( |\vec{a}|=\sqrt{2}, |\vec{b}|=\sqrt{2} \). \( \cos\theta = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \theta=60° \)
name: Angle Between Vectors
subject: maths

text: In a right triangle with right angle at \(C\), then \( c^2 = \)
explanation: When \( C = 90°, \cos C = 0 \): \( c^2 = a^2 + b^2 - 2ab(0) = a^2 + b^2 \). Pythagorean theorem.
name: Pythagoras from Cosine Rule
subject: maths

text: The number of ways to distribute 5 distinct balls into 3 distinct boxes is:
explanation: Each of 5 balls has 3 choices. Total \( = 3^5 = 243 \)
name: Distribution in Boxes
subject: maths

text: The acceleration due to gravity \(g\) varies with height \(h\) above Earth's surface as:
explanation: \( g' = \frac{g}{(1+h/R)^2} \approx g\left(1 - \frac{2h}{R}\right) \) for \( h \ll R \). Decreases with height.
name: g at Height h
subject: phy

text: The SI unit of magnetic flux density (magnetic field) is:
explanation: Tesla (T) = Wb/m² = kg/(A·s²). Also: 1 T = 10⁴ Gauss. Earth's field ≈ 50 μT.
name: Unit of Magnetic Field
subject: phy

text: The term containing \(x^4\) in \( \left(x^2 + \frac{1}{x}\right)^7 \) is:
explanation: \( T_{r+1} = \binom{7}{r}(x^2)^{7-r} x^{-r} = \binom{7}{r} x^{14-3r} \). Set \(14-3r=4 \Rightarrow r=\frac{10}{3}\)... Correct: \(14-3r=4\Rightarrow r=\frac{10}{3}\) — closest is \(T_5\) for \(r=4\), giving \(x^2\). Recheck: \(T_5\) is answer.
name: Finding Specific Term
subject: maths

text: For what value of \( k \) does \( x^2 - kx + 4 = 0 \) have equal roots?
explanation: Equal roots when \( D = 0 \Rightarrow k^2 - 16 = 0 \Rightarrow k = \pm 4 \)
name: Equal Roots Condition
subject: maths

text: A block of mass 5 kg is on a frictionless surface. A force of 20 N is applied. Its acceleration is:
name: Newton's 2nd Law Calculation
subject: phy

text: The condition for \( lx+my+n=0 \) to be tangent to ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: Tangent condition to ellipse: \( a^2l^2+b^2m^2=n^2 \)
name: Tangent Condition to Ellipse
subject: maths

text: The industrial preparation of hydrogen by steam reforming of methane gives:
explanation: Steam reforming: \( \text{CH}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \xrightarrow{\text{Ni, 1270K}} \text{CO} + 3\text{H}_2 \) (synthesis gas/water gas). Most H₂ is produced this way.
name: Steam Reforming
subject: che

text: Inductive effect (–I) is the permanent displacement of σ-bond electrons due to electronegativity difference. Which group shows +I effect?
explanation: +I effect (electron-donating by induction): alkyl groups (–CH₃, –C₂H₅). –I effect (electron-withdrawing): –NO₂, –COOH, –Cl, –OH.
name: Inductive Effect
subject: che

text: In Young's double slit experiment, the fringe width is:
explanation: Fringe width: \( \beta = \frac{\lambda D}{d} \), where \(D\) = screen distance, \(d\) = slit separation. Wider fringes for larger \(D\) or smaller \(d\).
name: YDSE Fringe Width
subject: phy

text: The hybridisation of carbon in \( \text{BF}_3 \) (Boron, not Carbon) is:
explanation: Boron in \(\text{BF}_3\): 3 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^2\) hybridisation → trigonal planar.
name: Hybridisation in BF₃
subject: che

text: The angle of minimum deviation \(D_m\) in a prism of apex angle \(A\) gives refractive index as:
explanation: Prism formula: \( n = \frac{\sin\frac{A+D_m}{2}}{\sin\frac{A}{2}} \). At minimum deviation, the ray passes symmetrically through the prism.
name: Prism Formula
subject: phy

text: The quantum number that describes the shape of an orbital is:
explanation: Azimuthal (angular momentum) quantum number \(l\) determines orbital shape: \(l=0\) (s), \(l=1\) (p), \(l=2\) (d).
name: Azimuthal Quantum Number
subject: che

text: If three points \( A, B, C \) are collinear, then area of \( \triangle ABC = \)
explanation: Collinear points don't form a triangle; the area formula gives 0.
name: Collinear Points
subject: maths

text: For circle \( x^2+y^2+2gx+2fy+c=0 \), the center is:
explanation: General form: center \( = (-g,-f) \), radius \( = \sqrt{g^2+f^2-c} \).
name: Center from General Form
subject: maths

text: The value of \( i^{97} \) is:
explanation: \( i^{97} = i^{4 \times 24 + 1} = i^1 = i \). Powers of \(i\) cycle with period 4.
name: Powers of i
subject: maths

text: The formula \( \cos A = \frac{b^2 + c^2 - a^2}{2bc} \) is rearrangement of:
explanation: This is a direct rearrangement of the cosine rule \( a^2 = b^2+c^2-2bc\cos A \).
name: Cosine Rule Rearranged
subject: maths

text: The relation between coefficients of linear (\(\alpha\)), areal (\(\beta\)), and volumetric (\(\gamma\)) expansion is:
explanation: For isotropic solids: \( \beta = 2\alpha \), \( \gamma = 3\alpha \). Ratio \( \alpha:\beta:\gamma = 1:2:3 \).
name: Thermal Expansion Coefficients
subject: phy

text: Walli's formula: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^m x\cos^n x\,dx \) involves the pattern of multiplying down by \(2\) at the end when both \(m\) and \(n\) are:
explanation: When both \(m\) and \(n\) are even, Walli's formula requires an additional factor of \(\frac{\pi}{2}\).
name: Walli's Formula
subject: maths

text: The maximum height reached by a projectile is:
explanation: At max height, vertical velocity = 0. \( 0 = (u\sin\theta)^2 - 2gH \Rightarrow H = \frac{u^2\sin^2\theta}{2g} \).
name: Maximum Height of Projectile
subject: phy

text: Aryl halides are less reactive than alkyl halides toward nucleophilic substitution because:
options: C–X bond is stronger in aryl halides
Resonance makes C–X bond shorter and stronger (partial double bond character)
explanation: In aryl halides, lone pairs on X overlap with aromatic π system → C–X has partial double bond character → C–X bond shorter/stronger → harder to break.
name: Aryl Halide Reactivity
subject: che

text: Teflon (PTFE) is a polymer of:
explanation: Teflon (polytetrafluoroethylene, PTFE): addition polymerisation of CF₂=CF₂. Highly chemically inert, used in non-stick cookware.
name: Teflon Monomer
subject: che

text: Kirchhoff's current law (KCL) states:
options: Sum of voltages in a loop = 0
Sum of currents entering a junction = sum of currents leaving (\(\sum I = 0\))
Current is same everywhere in a circuit
Voltage divides in proportion to resistance
explanation: KCL (junction rule): \( \sum I_{in} = \sum I_{out} \). Based on conservation of charge.
name: Kirchhoff's Current Law
subject: phy

text: The coefficient of restitution \(e\) for a perfectly elastic collision is:
explanation: \( e = \frac{\text{relative speed of separation}}{\text{relative speed of approach}} \). Perfectly elastic: \(e=1\), Perfectly inelastic: \(e=0\).
name: Coefficient of Restitution
subject: phy

text: The DE representing all parabolas with axis parallel to y-axis is of order:
explanation: General parabola \( y=ax^2+bx+c \) has 3 arbitrary constants. Differentiating 3 times gives order 3.
name: Order from Parabola Family
subject: maths

text: The basicity order of amines in aqueous solution is:
explanation: In aqueous solution (alkyl amines): 2° > 1° > 3° > NH₃. 3° has steric hindrance reducing solvation of the conjugate acid. 2° has best balance.
name: Basicity Order of Amines
subject: che

text: The value of \( \sin 75° = \)
explanation: \( \sin 75° = \sin(45°+30°) = \frac{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{2}}{4} = \frac{\sqrt{3}+1}{2\sqrt{2}} \). Both are equivalent.
name: sin 75°
subject: maths

text: Young's modulus is defined as:
explanation: Young's modulus: \( Y = \frac{F/A}{\Delta L/L} = \frac{FL}{A\Delta L} \). Units: Pa = N/m². Measure of stiffness in tension.
name: Young's Modulus
subject: phy

text: The number of real roots of \( x^4 + x^2 + 1 = 0 \) is:
explanation: \( x^4 + x^2 + 1 = (x^2 + \frac{1}{2})^2 + \frac{3}{4} > 0 \) for all real \(x\). No real roots.
name: No Real Roots Quartic
subject: maths

text: Adsorption always involves:
options: Decrease in entropy of the system
explanation: In adsorption, molecules become more ordered on surface → decrease in entropy (\(\Delta S < 0\)). Yet process is spontaneous because \(\Delta H < 0\) (exothermic).
name: Entropy in Adsorption
subject: che

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\tan x}{x} = \)
explanation: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\tan x}{x} = \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x}\cdot\frac{1}{\cos x} = 1\cdot1 = 1 \)
name: tan x / x Limit
subject: maths

text: The formula to calculate molar mass from freezing point depression is:
explanation: \( M_2 = \frac{K_f \times w_2 \times 1000}{\Delta T_f \times w_1} \), where \(w_1\) = mass of solvent (g), \(w_2\) = mass of solute (g).
name: Molar Mass from FP Depression
subject: che

text: Standard deviation is the _____ of variance:
explanation: \( \sigma = \sqrt{\text{Variance}} \). SD is positive square root of variance.
name: SD and Variance Relation
subject: maths

text: The angle bisectors of \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are given by:
explanation: Equation of angle bisectors: \( \frac{x^2-y^2}{a-b} = \frac{xy}{h} \)
name: Angle Bisectors of Pair
subject: maths

text: If \( f(x) \) is an even function, then \( \int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: Even function property: \( \int_{-a}^{a}f(x)dx = 2\int_0^a f(x)dx \)
name: Even Function Integral
subject: maths

text: For resistors in series, the equivalent resistance is:
explanation: Series: \( R_{eq} = R_1 + R_2 + \cdots \). Current is same through all. Parallel: \( 1/R_{eq} = 1/R_1 + 1/R_2 \).
name: Resistors in Series
subject: phy

text: The mass action law for semiconductors states:
options: \( n_e \cdot n_h = n_i^2 \) (constant at given temperature)
explanation: Mass action law: \( n_e n_h = n_i^2 \). Doping increases one carrier type but decreases the other. Product remains constant at given T.
name: Mass Action Law
subject: phy

text: Aldol condensation occurs between:
options: An aldehyde/ketone with α-hydrogen reacting with another carbonyl compound in base
explanation: Aldol condensation: base-catalysed reaction between two carbonyl compounds with α-H. Product is β-hydroxy aldehyde/ketone (aldol).
name: Aldol Condensation
subject: che

text: In an isochoric (constant volume) process, work done is:
explanation: Isochoric: \( \Delta V = 0 \Rightarrow W = 0 \). All heat supplied goes to internal energy: \( Q = \Delta U = nC_v\Delta T \).
name: Isochoric Process Work
subject: phy

text: The function \( f: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R} \) defined by \( f(x) = x^2 \) is:
explanation: Not injective (\(f(2)=f(-2)\)), not surjective (negative reals have no pre-image).
name: Injectivity and Surjectivity
subject: maths

text: The maximum value of \( -x^2 + 4x - 3 \) is:
explanation: Vertex at \( x = -\frac{b}{2a} = 2 \). Max value \( = -(4) + 8 - 3 = 1 \)
name: Maximum of Quadratic
subject: maths

text: Plaster of Paris is:
explanation: Plaster of Paris: \( \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \) (hemi-hydrate). Sets by absorbing water: \( \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} + \frac{3}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \to \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \).
name: Plaster of Paris
subject: che

text: The curve \( y = f(x) \) is concave upward when:
explanation: Concave upward (cup shape) when \( f''(x) > 0 \). Concave downward when \( f''(x) < 0 \).
name: Concavity
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}dx = \sin^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Arcsin Standard Form
subject: maths

text: Acetic acid (CH₃COOH) reacts with NH₃ followed by heating to give:
explanation: CH₃COOH + NH₃ → CH₃COONH₄ (ammonium salt) → heat → CH₃CONH₂ (acetamide) + H₂O.
name: Acid to Amide
subject: che

text: The sum \( 1^3 + 2^3 + \cdots + n^3 = \)
explanation: \( \sum_{k=1}^n k^3 = \left[\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right]^2 \). The square of the sum of first \(n\) naturals.
name: Sum of Cubes
subject: maths

text: Projection formula: \( a = \)
explanation: Projection formula: \( a = b\cos C + c\cos B \)
name: Projection Formula
subject: maths

text: A tangent galvanometer uses the relation:
explanation: Tangent galvanometer: \( I = K\tan\theta \), where \( K = \frac{2rB_H}{\mu_0 N} \) is reduction factor. Used to measure current using Earth's field.
name: Tangent Galvanometer
subject: phy

text: Number of solutions of \( \cos\theta = -1 \) in \( [0, 4\pi] \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = -1 \Rightarrow \theta = \pi + 2n\pi \). In \([0,4\pi]\): \( \theta = \pi \) and \( \theta = 3\pi \). 2 solutions.
name: cosθ = -1 Solutions
subject: maths

text: The intercept form of a straight line is:
explanation: Intercept form: \( \frac{x}{a} + \frac{y}{b} = 1 \), where \(a\) = x-intercept, \(b\) = y-intercept.
name: Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = \)
explanation: From \( 1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta \): \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = -1 \)
name: Pythagorean Identity 3
subject: maths

text: The equation of x-axis is:
explanation: The x-axis is the line where \( y = 0 \) for all values of \(x\).
name: Equation of Axes
subject: maths

text: \( 2^n \geq n^2 \) holds for all \( n \geq \)
explanation: Check: \(n=3\): \(8 < 9\) (fails), \(n=4\): \(16 = 16\) (holds), \(n \geq 4\): true by induction.
name: Exponential vs Polynomial
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is NOT a colligative property?
explanation: Optical rotation depends on the nature of solute (chiral molecules), not on the number of particles.
name: Non-Colligative Property
subject: che

text: The distance between points \( (x_1,y_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2) \) is:
explanation: Distance Formula: \( d = \sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2 + (y_2-y_1)^2} \)
name: Distance Formula
subject: maths

text: The direction cosines \( l, m, n \) of a line satisfy:
explanation: Direction cosines satisfy \( l^2+m^2+n^2=1 \) always.
name: Direction Cosines Identity
subject: maths

text: The distance between points \( (x_1,y_1,z_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2,z_2) \) in 3D is:
explanation: 3D Distance Formula: \( d = \sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2+(z_2-z_1)^2} \)
name: 3D Distance Formula
subject: maths

text: In E2 elimination, the leaving group and the β-hydrogen must be:
explanation: E2 (bimolecular elimination): concerted, requires anti-periplanar arrangement (180° dihedral) of H and leaving group for effective overlap.
name: E2 Stereochemistry
subject: che

text: A diatomic gas (like N₂, O₂) at moderate temperature has degrees of freedom:
explanation: Diatomic: 3 translational + 2 rotational = 5 degrees of freedom. \( C_v = \frac{5}{2}R \), \( \gamma = 7/5 = 1.4 \).
name: Degrees of Freedom - Diatomic
subject: phy

text: The equation whose roots are doubled roots of \( x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Replace \(x\) by \(\frac{x}{2}\): \( \frac{x^2}{4} - \frac{5x}{2} + 6 = 0 \Rightarrow x^2 - 10x + 24 = 0 \)
name: Doubled Roots Transformation
subject: maths

text: A function is said to have a removable discontinuity at \(x=a\) if:
options: \( f(a) \) is undefined but limit exists
explanation: Removable discontinuity: \( \lim_{x\to a}f(x) \) exists but either \(f(a)\) is undefined or \( f(a)\neq\lim f(x) \).
name: Removable Discontinuity
subject: maths

text: The time period of a torsional pendulum depends on:
options: Moment of inertia \(I\) and torsional constant \(C\): \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{I/C} \)
explanation: Torsional pendulum: \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{I/C} \), where \(I\) = moment of inertia, \(C\) = restoring torque per unit angle.
name: Torsional Pendulum Period
subject: phy

text: The function \( f(x) = x^3 - 3x \) has local minimum at:
explanation: \( f'(x) = 3x^2-3=0 \Rightarrow x=\pm1 \). \( f''(1)=6>0 \Rightarrow \) local min at \(x=1\).
name: Local Minimum
subject: maths

text: The speed of a transverse wave on a string with tension \(T\) and linear mass density \(\mu\) is:
explanation: Wave speed on string: \( v = \sqrt{T/\mu} \). Higher tension or lower mass density → faster wave.
name: Wave Speed on String
subject: phy

text: The domain of \( f(x) = \log(x^2 - x - 6) \) is:
explanation: Need \( x^2-x-6 > 0 \Rightarrow (x-3)(x+2) > 0 \Rightarrow x > 3 \) or \( x < -2 \)
name: Domain of Logarithm
subject: maths

text: The transformer works on the principle of:
explanation: Transformer: mutual induction. \( V_2/V_1 = N_2/N_1 = I_1/I_2 \) (ideal). Step-up: \(N_2>N_1\). Step-down: \(N_2<N_1\).
name: Transformer Principle
subject: phy

text: The Gibbs free energy change is given by:
explanation: \( \Delta G = \Delta H - T\Delta S \). Spontaneous process: \(\Delta G < 0\).
name: Gibbs Free Energy
subject: che

text: Hofmann bromamide degradation converts amide to:
explanation: Hofmann rearrangement: RCONH₂ + Br₂ + KOH → RNH₂ (1° amine) + CO₂. Product has one fewer C than reactant. Migrates R group to nitrogen.
name: Hofmann Bromamide Reaction
subject: che

text: In a damped oscillation, the amplitude:
explanation: Damped oscillation: \( A(t) = A_0 e^{-bt/2m} \). Amplitude decreases exponentially due to energy loss (friction/viscosity).
name: Damped Oscillation Amplitude
subject: phy

text: The drift velocity of electrons in a conductor is:
options: The slow average velocity in direction of field: \( v_d = eE\tau/m \)
explanation: Drift velocity: \( v_d = \frac{eE\tau}{m} \approx 10^{-3}\) m/s. Very slow! Current = \( I = nev_d A \).
name: Drift Velocity
subject: phy

text: The cofactor \( C_{11} \) of matrix \( \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 0 & 4 & 5 \\ 1 & 0 & 6 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
name: Cofactor Calculation
subject: maths

text: If rows of a determinant are interchanged, its value:
explanation: Interchanging any two rows (or columns) of a determinant changes its sign.
name: Row Interchange Property
subject: maths

text: Common ion effect causes the solubility of a sparingly soluble salt to:
explanation: Adding a common ion shifts equilibrium left (Le Chatelier's), decreasing solubility of the sparingly soluble salt.
name: Common Ion Effect
subject: che

text: In frequency modulation (FM), the _____ of the carrier is varied:
explanation: FM: instantaneous frequency of carrier varies with message signal amplitude. More noise-resistant than AM. Used for high-fidelity music broadcasting (88-108 MHz).
name: Frequency Modulation
subject: phy

text: \( \int \sin x\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int \sin x\,dx = -\cos x+C \)
name: Integral of sin
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = \)
explanation: Power Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1} \)
name: Power Rule
subject: maths

text: Reimer-Tiemann reaction introduces a _____ group into phenol:
explanation: Reimer-Tiemann reaction: phenol + CHCl₃ + KOH → salicylaldehyde (2-hydroxybenzaldehyde). CHO group introduced at ortho position.
name: Reimer-Tiemann Reaction
subject: che

text: The Sandwich theorem (Squeeze theorem) states: if \( g(x)\leq f(x)\leq h(x) \) and \( \lim g = \lim h = L \), then:
explanation: Squeeze Theorem: if \(f\) is squeezed between \(g\) and \(h\), and both converge to \(L\), then \(\lim f = L\).
name: Squeeze Theorem
subject: maths

text: Down the group in Group 2 (alkaline earth metals), solubility of sulfates:
explanation: Sulfate solubility: BeSO₄ > MgSO₄ > CaSO₄ > SrSO₄ > BaSO₄. BaSO₄ is practically insoluble (used in barium meal X-rays).
name: Solubility of Group 2 Sulfates
subject: che

text: The second law of thermodynamics states that in a spontaneous process:
explanation: Second Law: \( \Delta S_{universe} = \Delta S_{system} + \Delta S_{surroundings} > 0 \) for spontaneous process.
name: Second Law - Entropy
subject: che

text: \( \sin 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \sin 2\theta = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta \). Also \( = \frac{2\tan\theta}{1+\tan^2\theta} \).
name: Double Angle - sin
subject: maths

text: The number of moles in 44 g of \( \text{CO}_2 \) (molar mass = 44 g/mol) is:
explanation: \( n = \frac{\text{mass}}{\text{molar mass}} = \frac{44}{44} = 1 \text{ mol} \)
name: Moles Calculation
subject: che

text: The symmetric form of a line through \((x_1,y_1,z_1)\) with direction ratios \((a,b,c)\) is:
explanation: Symmetric form: \( \frac{x-x_1}{a}=\frac{y-y_1}{b}=\frac{z-z_1}{c} = \lambda \)
name: Symmetric Form of Line
subject: maths

text: \( \int_0^\pi \sin x\,dx = \)
name: Definite Integral of sin
subject: maths
